Adult White Dragon.signature: The least intelligent adult dragon (INT 8) with Ice Walk — fights like a cunning animal in blizzard conditions where the party is blinded and freezing.
abilities: Cold Breath (Recharge 5-6): 12d8 cold in a 60-ft cone. CON DC 19. High damage targeting CON.
Frightful Presence: WIS DC 14. Lower DC than other adults — reflects its simpler nature.
Ice Walk: Moves on ice without difficulty. The dragon's home terrain is treacherous for the party.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Adult White Dragon.tactical_identity: Savage predator that fights like an apex animal in frozen terrain. Uses blizzard conditions, ice, and cold to advantage. Less tactical than other dragons but compensates with environmental mastery.
encounter_hooks: Ice Walk in its lair means the dragon moves freely while the party slips on ice (difficult terrain)
INT 8 means it doesn't negotiate or scheme — it's a territorial predator that attacks trespassers
arctic lair hazards: freezing water, avalanches, ice bridges, blinding blizzards — the environment is the encounter
Adult White Dragon.description: A huge dragon of pure white with ice-blue eyes, radiating a killing cold. The most bestial adult dragon — cunning enough to set ambushes but driven primarily by hunger and territorial fury.
Adult White Dragon.retreat: Flies across frozen terrain where pursuit is nearly impossible.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon's lair is a frozen tomb of previous victims — preserved perfectly in ice for decades
the cold aura doesn't fade immediately — the lair remains supernaturally cold for weeks
the dragon was the apex predator keeping other arctic threats in check — with it gone, those threats emerge

Adult Gold Dragon.signature: Weakening Breath + Change Shape + Legendary Actions make the adult gold dragon a shape-shifting warrior that cripples martial characters while maintaining full combat effectiveness.
abilities: Fire Breath: 12d10 fire in a 60-ft cone. DEX DC 21. Massive fire damage.
Weakening Breath: 60-ft cone, STR DC 21 or weapon damage halved. Devastates martial characters.
Change Shape: Polymorphs into any humanoid or beast. Can infiltrate, spy, or approach in disguise.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Adult Gold Dragon.tactical_identity: Noble champion that fights with honor but overwhelming power. Uses Weakening Breath to neutralize fighters, then engages in melee where its Multiattack dominates weakened opponents. May test the party rather than try to kill them.
encounter_hooks: Change Shape means the party might already know this dragon in humanoid form — the reveal is the encounter
Weakening Breath halves the party's weapon damage — suddenly the dragon's HP pool is effectively doubled
gold dragons are lawful good — combat with one means the party has done something wrong, or it's a test
Adult Gold Dragon.description: An immense dragon with scales of pure gold and eyes that radiate ancient wisdom. The mightiest of the good dragons — may appear as a humble traveler to test mortals.
Adult Gold Dragon.retreat: Retreats only to protect something more important. Returns with allies and purpose.
Adult Gold Dragon.social: The wisest and most just of dragons. Takes humanoid form frequently. May have been watching the party in disguise for days. Offers aid to worthy causes.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon was preparing for a specific apocalyptic threat — its research and preparations are now critical
the dragon had allies across the continent — its network of contacts becomes available to the party
the dragon's hoard includes artifacts it was safeguarding — each one was kept from the wrong hands for a reason

Adult Silver Dragon.signature: Paralyzing Breath with a DC 20 CON save can freeze the entire party — combined with Legendary Actions, a silver dragon can paralyze and then systematically dismantle helpless characters.
abilities: Cold Breath: 13d8 cold in a 60-ft cone. CON DC 20. Very high damage.
Paralyzing Breath: 60-ft cone, CON DC 20 or paralyzed for 1 minute. At this DC, most party members will fail.
Change Shape: Polymorphs into any humanoid or beast. Silver dragons frequently live among humanoids.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Adult Silver Dragon.tactical_identity: Benevolent protector that lives among humanoids in disguise. Fights only to protect — but when it does, Paralyzing Breath instantly removes most threats. Prefers to end fights without killing.
encounter_hooks: Paralyzing Breath at DC 20 paralyzes most party members — the dragon can end a fight in one breath
frequently disguised as a humanoid in a nearby town — an NPC the party knows might be a dragon
silver dragons protect humanoid communities — the party fighting one means they're the threat
Adult Silver Dragon.description: A magnificent dragon with mirror-bright scales and a regal but warm demeanor. Deeply fond of humanoid company — likely to be encountered in humanoid form, living as a noble or scholar.
Adult Silver Dragon.retreat: Polymorphs into a humanoid and disappears into a settlement.
Adult Silver Dragon.social: Lives among humanoids by choice. Maintains deep friendships. Will reveal its true form to defend those it loves.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon's humanoid identity is prominent in a settlement — a leader, advisor, or beloved figure
the dragon's adopted community doesn't know it was a dragon — the revelation changes everything
the dragon was protecting its community from a threat they didn't know existed — that threat is now unguarded

Adult Brass Dragon.signature: Sleep Breath in a 60-ft cone can put the entire party to sleep — an adult brass dragon ends fights without bloodshed and interrogates unconscious captives.
abilities: Fire Breath: 14d6 fire in a 60-ft line. Damage option when peaceful resolution fails.
Sleep Breath: 60-ft cone, CON DC 16 or unconscious for 10 minutes. Nonlethal win condition.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Legendary Resistance (3/Day).
Adult Brass Dragon.tactical_identity: Compulsive conversationalist that talks endlessly, puts intruders to sleep with Sleep Breath, then continues the conversation with their unconscious bodies. Only uses fire when genuinely angered.
encounter_hooks: Sleep Breath means the encounter might end with the party waking up in a different location — alive but relocated
brass dragons talk incessantly — the party might get useful information if they listen patiently
a brass dragon's idea of hospitality is sleep-breathing guests and keeping them as 'company'
Adult Brass Dragon.description: A large dragon with dull gold scales, perpetually surrounded by desert heat and an unstoppable desire to talk. Will corner travelers for hours of conversation — combat is a last resort.
Adult Brass Dragon.retreat: Flies away mid-sentence. Returns to finish the story it was telling.
Adult Brass Dragon.social: The most talkative creature alive. Shares secrets freely. Genuinely good-natured. Uses Sleep Breath on rude guests rather than killing them.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon's knowledge of the region is encyclopedic — its notes and maps are invaluable
the dragon had been protecting a desert community from sandstorms using its weather-affecting abilities
its hoard is curated like a museum — each item has a story the dragon had memorized

Adult Bronze Dragon.signature: Repulsion Breath pushes creatures 60 feet — an adult bronze dragon can hurl the entire party off a coastal cliff or into the ocean with one breath.
abilities: Lightning Breath: 12d10 lightning in a 90-ft line. High damage, extreme range.
Repulsion Breath: 30-ft cone, STR DC 19 or pushed 60 feet. No damage but catastrophic repositioning near edges or water.
Change Shape: Polymorphs into humanoid or beast. Often takes humanoid form to observe coastal settlements.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Adult Bronze Dragon.tactical_identity: Coastal guardian that patrols waterways and protects sailors. Fights near water where Repulsion Breath can push enemies into the ocean. Uses humanoid form to investigate threats before engaging as a dragon.
encounter_hooks: Repulsion Breath near a cliff or ocean edge is effectively a kill shot — 60 feet of forced movement into open water
bronze dragons protect coastal communities — fighting one means the party is a threat to innocents
Change Shape lets it approach as a helpful stranger before revealing itself
Adult Bronze Dragon.description: A powerful coastal dragon with dark bronze scales weathered by salt spray. An active defender of the coastline who may recruit adventurers for missions against sea threats.
Adult Bronze Dragon.retreat: Dives into the ocean. Patrols from underwater.
Adult Bronze Dragon.social: A warrior and strategist. Aids just causes, especially at sea. Transforms into humanoid form to walk among sailors.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon was tracking a major sea-based threat — pirates, a kraken, or a sahuagin army
its coastal allies depended on its naval protection — that coast is now vulnerable
the dragon had a humanoid identity in a port city — contacts there trust that identity

Adult Copper Dragon.signature: Slowing Breath in a 60-ft cone cripples the party's action economy — halved speed, no reactions, one attack maximum. The party becomes sitting ducks for follow-up attacks.
abilities: Acid Breath: 12d8 acid in a 60-ft line. Standard damage breath.
Slowing Breath: 60-ft cone, CON DC 18 or slowed (halved speed, no reactions, max one attack, limited spellcasting) for 1 minute.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Legendary Resistance (3/Day).
Adult Copper Dragon.tactical_identity: Playful trickster that finds combat amusing. Uses Slowing Breath to humiliate enemies, then toys with them while they stumble around at half speed unable to react.
encounter_hooks: Slowing Breath removes reactions — no Shield, no Counterspell, no Opportunity Attacks, no Absorb Elements
copper dragons are pranksters — the encounter might be a series of escalating pranks that become dangerous
good-aligned but irreverent — may help the party if entertained, or prank them if bored
Adult Copper Dragon.description: A large dragon with gleaming reddish-copper scales, grinning constantly. A trickster who values a good joke over treasure — may help the party if they're funny enough.
Adult Copper Dragon.retreat: Flees through mountain caves using climb speed. Leaves collapsing tunnels behind.
Adult Copper Dragon.social: Riddler supreme. Every serious conversation devolves into jokes. Offers real help disguised as pranks.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon's lair is a puzzle dungeon it built for entertainment — the real treasure requires solving it
the dragon kept a bully or tyrant in check through pranks — that threat returns immediately
the dragon's jokes contained encoded warnings about a real danger — decoded, they form a prophecy

Ancient Red Dragon.signature: Fire Breath dealing 26d6 (average 91) in a 90-ft cone is potentially the deadliest single action in the game — an ancient red dragon IS the apocalypse scenario.
abilities: Fire Breath (Recharge 5-6): 26d6 fire in a 90-ft cone. DEX DC 24. Average 91 damage. Can kill most characters outright.
Frightful Presence: WIS DC 21. Nearly impossible to resist — most of the party will be frightened.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Legendary Resistance (3/Day): Auto-succeeds three saves.
Ancient Red Dragon.tactical_identity: World-ending apex predator that opens by scattering the party with Frightful Presence, then incinerates the largest group. Wing Attack repositions it constantly. Fights to the death because nothing should be able to threaten it.
encounter_hooks: 91 average fire damage kills most characters outright — fire resistance is mandatory preparation
DC 24 breath save means even high-DEX characters fail regularly — Evasion rogues are the exception
an ancient red dragon fight is a campaign climax — the party needs specific preparation, allies, and strategy
Ancient Red Dragon.description: A dragon the size of a castle with scales of darkened iron and a furnace glow visible through cracks in its hide. The apex predator of the material plane — everything it sees is either treasure, food, or beneath notice.
Ancient Red Dragon.retreat: Flies away in volcanic fury. The retreat itself causes destruction. Returns to burn everything.
Ancient Red Dragon.social: The apex predator of all dragonkind. Demands absolute subjugation. Converses only because it amuses itself. Every word is a threat.
aftermath_hooks: the hoard is legendary in scope — its redistribution reshapes regional economics
the volcanic lair destabilizes without the dragon's presence — eruption is possible within weeks
the dragon's death is a world event — factions race to claim territory, hoard, and lair

Ancient Blue Dragon.signature: Lightning Breath in a 120-ft line can hit the entire party from over 100 feet away — an ancient blue dragon fights from extreme range where most parties can't effectively respond.
abilities: Lightning Breath (Recharge 5-6): 16d10 lightning in a 120-ft line. DEX DC 23. Extreme range — can hit from beyond most spell ranges.
Frightful Presence: WIS DC 20.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Legendary Resistance (3/Day).
Ancient Blue Dragon.tactical_identity: Desert emperor that rules through fear and dominance. Strafes from maximum range, never landing unless certain of victory. Commands lesser creatures as an army — the dragon is the general, not just a monster.
encounter_hooks: 120-ft line breath outranges most party attacks — the dragon can strafe without retaliation
rules an empire of desert creatures — the encounter includes minions, traps, and fortified positions
the most egotistical dragon type — can be manipulated through its vanity but the cost of failure is death
Ancient Blue Dragon.description: A titanic dragon with deep indigo scales and horns like a crown of lightning rods, commanding desert storms. A self-proclaimed deity that demands worship and tribute from all living things in its domain.
Ancient Blue Dragon.retreat: Flies to a secondary lair prepared for exactly this situation.
Ancient Blue Dragon.social: An emperor in all but title. Commands nations through fear and tribute. May negotiate because it finds the party amusing.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon's network of tribute-paying settlements and subjugated rulers collapses — the political fallout is immense
the ancient ruin beneath its lair was nearly fully excavated — what it was digging for is almost exposed
the dragon had a rival ancient dragon — with one dead, the other makes an immediate territorial move

Ancient Green Dragon.signature: INT 20 + Poison Breath in a 90-ft cone + centuries of accumulated schemes — an ancient green dragon is the most intelligent and manipulative creature most parties will ever face.
abilities: Poison Breath (Recharge 5-6): 22d6 poison in a 90-ft cone. CON DC 22. Wide cone, massive damage.
Frightful Presence: WIS DC 19.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Legendary Resistance (3/Day).
Ancient Green Dragon.tactical_identity: Master manipulator who has been scheming for centuries. Prefers to destroy the party through politics, blackmail, and turned allies long before resorting to combat. When it fights, it has already won.
encounter_hooks: INT 20 means it has planned for every contingency — fighting it in its lair is fighting on its terms
the dragon's network of spies, charmed agents, and blackmailed allies is more dangerous than its breath
an ancient green dragon encounter might span sessions of social intrigue before combat ever happens
Ancient Green Dragon.description: An enormous dragon with scales of deepest emerald, its breath a cloud of corrosive poison. The most patient and manipulative of all dragons — it has spent centuries building a web of agents and schemes.
Ancient Green Dragon.retreat: Vanishes into ancient forest. Its web of control persists without it.
Ancient Green Dragon.social: Has manipulated kingdoms for centuries. Every alliance it offers has been planned for decades. The truth and lies are indistinguishable.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon's political web spans nations — agents in courts, guilds, and temples don't know their master is dead
the forest the dragon controlled for centuries begins to transform — ancient magic unravels
the dragon's hoard is predominantly information — maps, contracts, secrets worth more than gold

Ancient White Dragon.signature: The least intelligent ancient dragon (INT 10) but Cold Breath in a 90-ft cone dealing 16d8 cold — what it lacks in cunning it compensates with raw predatory power in frozen environments.
abilities: Cold Breath (Recharge 5-6): 16d8 cold in a 90-ft cone. CON DC 22. Devastating damage.
Ice Walk: Unhindered movement on ice and snow.
Frightful Presence: WIS DC 16. Lower than other ancients.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Ancient White Dragon.tactical_identity: Apex arctic predator with just enough intelligence (INT 10) to use its lair and territory effectively. Fights in blizzards and on ice sheets where the party is blinded and the dragon moves freely.
encounter_hooks: the lair IS the weapon — avalanches, thin ice over freezing water, blinding blizzards, freezing fog
INT 10 makes it smarter than a beast but dumber than a schemer — it uses terrain instinctively, not strategically
the frozen lair contains flash-frozen victims from centuries of hunting — some may be preserved NPCs
Ancient White Dragon.description: A colossal dragon of glacial white, scarred by centuries of combat, its presence dropping the temperature to lethal cold. The oldest white dragons develop a cruel cunning that belies their bestial reputation.
Ancient White Dragon.retreat: Flies across arctic wastes. The terrain itself is its ally.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon's lair is a glacier containing centuries of frozen victims and treasure
the arctic ecosystem shifts without the apex predator — other creatures fill the void rapidly
the dragon's death thaws a section of permanent ice — what was preserved beneath is ancient

Ancient Black Dragon.signature: Acid Breath in a 90-ft line from the most sadistic ancient dragon — an ancient black dragon enjoys the suffering and destroys slowly rather than killing quickly.
abilities: Acid Breath (Recharge 5-6): 15d8 acid in a 90-ft line. DEX DC 22. Line breath that dissolves everything.
Frightful Presence: WIS DC 19.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Legendary Resistance (3/Day).
Ancient Black Dragon.tactical_identity: Sadistic torturer that could kill the party quickly but chooses not to. Toys with prey, retreats to let them heal, then attacks again. Its lair is designed to demoralize — bones, acid pools, trophies of previous victims.
encounter_hooks: swamp lair with acid pools, murky water, and no dry ground — the party fights in the dragon's element
sadistic nature means it might capture rather than kill — prisoners in its lair create rescue scenarios
an ancient black dragon's territory is a dead zone — no other creatures survive nearby, which telegraphs its presence
Ancient Black Dragon.description: A gargantuan dragon with pitted, acid-scarred scales and horns swept back like a crown of ruin. Centuries of cruelty have made it paranoid and vindictive — it hoards grudges alongside treasure.
Ancient Black Dragon.retreat: Retreats into its labyrinthine swamp lair. The swamp itself defends it.
Ancient Black Dragon.social: Ancient malice personified. Offers nothing. Takes everything. May speak only to promise suffering.
aftermath_hooks: the swamp begins to recede — the dragon's presence was what sustained it, and the ecology transforms over months
the hoard contains artifacts from fallen civilizations the dragon personally destroyed
the dragon's death releases creatures and spirits it had imprisoned in its swamp for centuries

Ancient Brass Dragon.signature: Sleep Breath in a 90-ft cone with centuries of stories to tell — an ancient brass dragon is the most likely ancient dragon to not fight at all, instead talking for hours and putting bored guests to sleep.
abilities: Fire Breath: 16d6 fire in a 90-ft line. Used only if genuinely threatened.
Sleep Breath: 90-ft cone, CON DC 18 or unconscious for 10 minutes. At this range, the entire party is affected.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Legendary Resistance (3/Day).
Ancient Brass Dragon.tactical_identity: Ancient storyteller that desperately wants company. Sleep Breathes guests who try to leave, then continues talking. Fights seriously only if its hoard or home is threatened — otherwise it's the world's most powerful extrovert.
encounter_hooks: the encounter might be escaping a conversation, not winning a fight — the dragon puts you to sleep if you try to leave
centuries of knowledge means it can answer almost any question — if the party endures the rambling
brass dragons collect stories — offering a genuinely novel tale might be worth more than gold
Ancient Brass Dragon.description: A towering dragon with warm, sandy-gold scales and an encyclopedic memory of conversations spanning millennia. The friendliest ancient dragon — but its Sleep Breath can end a conversation it's bored with.
Ancient Brass Dragon.retreat: Flies away still talking. May not even realize the fight is serious.
Ancient Brass Dragon.social: Has spoken with every notable figure in the region over centuries. Knows everything about everyone. Shares freely but rambles.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon's memory spans millennia — its recorded histories rewrite what the world thinks it knows
the dragon had promises and debts with beings across the planes — some of those come due now
desert communities protected by the dragon for generations must now fend for themselves

Ancient Bronze Dragon.signature: Repulsion Breath pushes creatures 60 feet with a STR DC 25 — almost nothing resists this, and near coastal cliffs or open ocean it's a death sentence.
abilities: Lightning Breath: 16d10 lightning in a 120-ft line. Extreme range, extreme damage.
Repulsion Breath: 30-ft cone, STR DC 25 or pushed 60 feet. Nearly impossible to resist.
Change Shape: Any humanoid or beast. Lives among coastal peoples.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Ancient Bronze Dragon.tactical_identity: Ancient guardian of coastal civilizations that fights to protect, not conquer. Repulsion Breath near water is its signature — enemies are hurled into the ocean. In humanoid form, it has been watching the party for a long time.
encounter_hooks: STR DC 25 Repulsion Breath means even the strongest fighters get pushed 60 feet — near water this is fatal
has been disguised as a coastal elder, lighthouse keeper, or harbor master for decades
protects ships and sailors — pirates and sea monsters are its enemies, and it may recruit the party
Ancient Bronze Dragon.description: A massive coastal dragon with barnacle-crusted bronze scales and a commanding, militaristic bearing. An active force for good that may recruit or test adventurers for a specific mission.
Ancient Bronze Dragon.retreat: Dives to oceanic depths. Returns with a strategy.
Ancient Bronze Dragon.social: The ultimate naval ally. Has fought sea wars for millennia. Offers strategic alliance against oceanic or coastal threats.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon was the last defense against a deep-sea threat — a kraken, aboleth, or dragon turtle now moves freely
its centuries of sunken treasure recovery have created a hoard of historical significance
the dragon's humanoid allies across port cities form a network the party can inherit

Ancient Copper Dragon.signature: Slowing Breath in a 90-ft cone at CON DC 22 cripples the entire party's action economy — halved speed, no reactions, maximum one attack per turn, limited casting.
abilities: Acid Breath: 14d8 acid in a 90-ft line.
Slowing Breath: 90-ft cone, CON DC 22 or slowed for 1 minute. Party-wide debuff that's devastating.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Legendary Resistance (3/Day).
Ancient Copper Dragon.tactical_identity: Ancient prankster who has been pulling jokes on mortals for centuries. Uses Slowing Breath to make enemies helpless and ridiculous rather than dead. Genuinely dangerous when angry but prefers to laugh.
encounter_hooks: Slowing Breath removes the party's ability to react — no Shield, Counterspell, or Opportunity Attacks for the whole group
centuries of pranking means its lair is full of traps designed to embarrass, not kill
the most approachable ancient dragon — might become an ally if the party can make it laugh
Ancient Copper Dragon.description: An enormous dragon with patinated copper scales and a grin that never fades. Its sense of humor has matured over millennia into something subtle and devastating — it enjoys ironic justice.
Ancient Copper Dragon.retreat: Causes a rockslide or cave collapse to cover escape. Thinks it's funny.
Ancient Copper Dragon.social: The oldest prankster alive. Its jokes contain prophecies. Its riddles have saved kingdoms. Impossible to have a serious conversation with.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon's mountain lair is the greatest puzzle dungeon ever built — generations of adventurers have tried and failed
the dragon's final riddle, posed during the encounter, is the key to something the party needs
the dragon was the only creature keeping a mountain pass safe through humor and misdirection

Ancient Gold Dragon.signature: Weakening Breath at STR DC 24 halves all weapon damage from affected creatures — combined with the highest breath damage (13d10 fire), an ancient gold dragon fights with overwhelming force tempered by mercy.
abilities: Fire Breath: 13d10 fire in a 90-ft cone. DEX DC 24. Average 71 damage — lethal to most.
Weakening Breath: 90-ft cone, STR DC 24 or weapon damage halved for 1 minute. Neutralizes martial characters completely.
Change Shape: Any humanoid or beast.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Ancient Gold Dragon.tactical_identity: The most powerful good-aligned dragon. Uses Weakening Breath to neutralize threats without killing, then negotiates from a position of absolute strength. Fights to the death only to protect the innocent.
encounter_hooks: Weakening Breath halves ALL weapon damage from the party — effectively doubles the dragon's HP against martial characters
in humanoid form, has been guiding or testing the party throughout the campaign
fighting an ancient gold dragon means the party has become a threat to the world — or the dragon is testing them
Ancient Gold Dragon.description: The largest and most powerful of the metallic dragons, with scales of pure burnished gold and an aura of divine authority. May be encountered in humanoid form as a wise ruler or humble sage.
Ancient Gold Dragon.retreat: Does not retreat unless the stakes demand it. Returns with divine-level allies.
Ancient Gold Dragon.social: Speaks with the authority of ages. May have walked among humanoids in multiple identities over centuries. Offers the party a role in a cosmic plan.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon was preparing for an event of world-changing scale — its preparations are incomplete and critical
the dragon's allies include gods, celestials, and ancient organizations — they will want to know what happened
the dragon's hoard is a vault of world-ending artifacts it was keeping from mortal hands — someone has to take over guardianship

Ancient Silver Dragon.signature: Paralyzing Breath at CON DC 24 freezes the entire party — almost nobody makes this save, and a paralyzed party is helpless against Legendary Action attacks.
abilities: Cold Breath: 15d8 cold in a 90-ft cone. CON DC 24. Devastating cold damage.
Paralyzing Breath: 90-ft cone, CON DC 24 or paralyzed for 1 minute. At this DC, the entire party fails. Paralysis = auto-crit.
Change Shape: Any humanoid or beast. Lives among humanoids more than any other dragon.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (costs 2).
Ancient Silver Dragon.tactical_identity: The most humanoid-friendly dragon, living disguised among mortals for decades. In combat, Paralyzing Breath instantly ends most fights — the dragon can then choose mercy or destruction.
encounter_hooks: CON DC 24 Paralyzing Breath paralyzes nearly everyone — the dragon wins initiative and the fight is over
the silver dragon has been a beloved community member in humanoid form — revealing it creates upheaval
fights only to protect its community — if the party threatens innocents, the dragon ends it instantly
Ancient Silver Dragon.description: A breathtaking dragon with scales like liquid mercury, radiating cold and benevolence in equal measure. The most likely ancient dragon to have humanoid friends, families, even whole communities it protects in disguise.
Ancient Silver Dragon.retreat: Polymorphs and vanishes into a humanoid population. Has maintained identities for centuries.
Ancient Silver Dragon.social: Has lived among humanoids longer than most kingdoms have existed. Its humanoid relationships span generations. Reveals itself only in moments of great need.
aftermath_hooks: the dragon's humanoid identity was a founder, leader, or institution — its loss reshapes a community
the dragon maintained secrets about the humanoid world that die with it unless its journal is found
the dragon's true friends — who knew what it was — form a society that now approaches the party

Balor.signature: Death Throes explodes for 20d6 fire damage in a 30-ft radius when it dies — killing a balor punishes the party for winning. Every melee character near it when it falls takes massive damage.
abilities: Death Throes: On death, explodes for 20d6 fire in 30 ft. DEX DC 20 for half. Average 70 damage — can kill characters who just barely won.
Multiattack: Longsword (3d8+8 slashing + 3d6 lightning) + whip (2d6+8 slashing + 3d6 fire, 30-ft reach, pulls target 25 ft closer). Devastating dual-weapon output.
Fire Aura: 3d6 fire damage to any creature starting its turn within 5 ft or touching the balor. Melee is painful.
Teleport: 120 ft teleportation. Repositions at will.
Balor.tactical_identity: Walking apocalypse that punishes melee engagement with Fire Aura, pulls ranged characters in with its 30-ft whip, and explodes when killed. The party must plan for the death explosion before committing to the kill.
encounter_hooks: Death Throes means the killing blow must come from range — melee characters near it when it dies take 70 average fire damage
Fire Aura + whip pull means nowhere is safe — ranged characters get yanked into the fire, melee characters burn
a balor is a campaign-climax demon — its presence means a portal to the Abyss or a summoning gone wrong
Balor.description: A towering, flame-wreathed demon with vast bat wings and a whip of fire, standing twenty feet tall. The generals of the Abyss — when a balor appears, a demonic army is not far behind.
Balor.retreat: Teleports away if outmatched. Its Death Throes explosion is the real final threat — 20d6 fire in 30 feet.
Balor.social: Commands with absolute authority. May offer a pact of mutual destruction against a shared enemy. Every deal is a trap leading to servitude.
aftermath_hooks: Death Throes — the explosion may have collapsed part of the environment, revealing or sealing something
the balor was commanding lesser demons — its death creates a power vacuum and the remaining demons turn on each other
a summoning circle or anchor binds the balor to this plane — destroying it prevents re-summoning

Marilith.signature: Six longsword attacks per turn with Reactive (seven reactions per round for opportunity attacks) — a marilith is a melee blender that punishes any movement within its reach.
abilities: Multiattack: Six longsword attacks + one tail attack per turn. Seven attacks total — the highest sustained melee output of any SRD creature.
Reactive: Seven reactions per round, one per turn. Can make opportunity attacks against SEVEN different creatures that leave its reach.
Teleport: 120 ft. Repositions to wherever melee is most advantageous.
Parry: +5 AC against one melee attack as a reaction. Uses one of its seven reactions to survive hits.
Marilith.tactical_identity: Melee zone controller that plants itself in the middle of the party and attacks everyone simultaneously. Seven reactions mean no one can move through or away from it safely. Teleports to reposition if the party spreads out.
encounter_hooks: seven reactions for opportunity attacks means no one can move away without getting hit — the marilith creates a kill zone
six longsword attacks per turn means even high-AC characters take hits through volume — healing can't keep up
Teleport lets it appear in the middle of the backline — nowhere is safe from 7+ attacks per round
Marilith.description: A six-armed serpentine demon wielding a weapon in each hand, her upper body that of a beautiful woman. A brilliant tactician among demons — she fights with calculated precision, not chaos.
Marilith.retreat: Teleports away when severely wounded. Plans a more devastating attack next time.
Marilith.social: A general, not a brute. Negotiates from a position of tactical superiority. May offer temporary alliance against a greater demon.
aftermath_hooks: the marilith commanded a demon army — battle plans in its lair reveal the next objective
its six swords each bear a different enchantment or marking — one sword has a history the party can trace
the marilith served a demon lord — its defeat is noticed, and a replacement is already being sent

Glabrezu.signature: Innate Spellcasting includes Power Word Stun and can grant a Wish — the glabrezu is a tempter demon that offers desires in exchange for corruption. The encounter might not involve combat at all.
abilities: Innate Spellcasting: Darkness, Detect Magic, Dispel Magic, Fly, Power Word Stun (1/day). Can grant one Wish per mortal (1/month) as a corruption tool.
Multiattack: Two pincer attacks. Each pincer grapples on hit (escape DC 15) and can auto-damage grappled creatures.
Power Word Stun: Stuns a target with 150 HP or less. No save. One of the best single-target disables in the game.
Glabrezu.tactical_identity: Tempter demon that prefers corruption over combat. Offers Wish to greedy mortals, then twists the result. When it fights, Power Word Stun disables a key target while pincers grapple and crush another.
encounter_hooks: the glabrezu's Wish offer is the real encounter — it corrupts the wisher, not grants true desires
Power Word Stun has no save — any character under 150 HP just stops functioning
as a tempter demon, it knows the party's desires and will offer exactly what they want most
Glabrezu.description: A massive, four-armed demon with pincers the size of a man and a face full of curved fangs. The tempter — it offers wishes and deals to corrupt mortals, then collects when they fail.
Glabrezu.retreat: Teleports to safety. Returns with a more elaborate scheme.
Glabrezu.social: The wish-granter. Offers the party exactly what they want — power, knowledge, resurrection — and the price is always more than they realize.
aftermath_hooks: a wish was granted before the party arrived — the consequences of that wish are unfolding in the world
the glabrezu's victim signed a contract that is still binding — the document is here, and it's legally airtight
the demon was summoned by a mortal who lost control — that summoner is nearby, terrified and hiding

Hezrou.signature: Stench poisons every creature within 10 feet that fails a CON save — the hezrou is a walking debuff zone that makes nearby creatures fight with disadvantage on attacks.
abilities: Stench: CON DC 14 or poisoned until start of next turn. Every creature within 10 ft at start of their turn. Poisoned = disadvantage on attacks and ability checks.
Multiattack: Bite + two claws. Solid melee damage output.
Magic Resistance: Advantage on saves against spells. Hard to disable with magic.
Hezrou.tactical_identity: Frontline bruiser that wades into melee and debilitates nearby creatures with Stench. The party's melee fighters are poisoned (disadvantage) just for standing near it. Magic Resistance makes it hard to bypass with spells.
encounter_hooks: Stench means melee fighters attack with disadvantage — ranged characters become the primary damage dealers
Magic Resistance + CON-based Stench DC means both spells and Constitution saves are unreliable against it
as a tanky melee demon, it's often paired with spellcasting demons that exploit the party's poisoned condition
Hezrou.description: A bloated, toad-like demon that reeks of rotting garbage and toxic bile. Stupid, strong, and surrounded by a stench so foul it incapacitates anyone nearby.
aftermath_hooks: the hezrou's stench has contaminated the area — the poisoned air lingers for hours
the demon was a foot soldier in a larger force — more demons are nearby or arriving
the summoning that brought it here is incomplete — the portal is still partially open

Vrock.signature: Spores deal ongoing poison damage that accumulates, and Stunning Screech deafens and stuns in a 20-ft radius — vrocks debilitate the party with area effects before closing to melee.
abilities: Spores (Recharge 6): 15-ft radius, CON DC 14 or 1d10 poison per turn for 1 minute. Damage stacks if multiple vrocks spore — in groups they're lethal.
Stunning Screech (1/Day): 20-ft radius, CON DC 14 or stunned until end of next turn. Stun the whole party, then attack.
Multiattack: Two attacks (talons and beak). Solid melee output.
Magic Resistance: Advantage on saves against spells.
Vrock.tactical_identity: Aerial skirmisher that opens with Stunning Screech to stun the party, drops Spores on clumped targets for ongoing damage, then swoops for melee. In groups, Spore damage stacks and becomes lethal.
encounter_hooks: Stunning Screech + Spores combo stuns the party then applies ongoing poison — devastating opening
multiple vrocks stacking Spores can deal 3d10+ poison per turn to the whole party
fly speed means it attacks from above and retreats out of melee reach between strikes
Vrock.description: A vulture-headed demon with massive wings and a shriek that stuns the living. It spreads toxic spores and fights with bestial savagery — more animal than fiend.
Vrock.retreat: Flies away when wounded. Circles overhead waiting for weakness.
aftermath_hooks: spores from the vrock's Spore attack are still growing on an infected character — they'll burst in 24 hours without treatment
the vrock's screech attracted other predators to the area
the demon was summoned as a distraction — the real objective is happening somewhere else right now

Dretch.signature: Fetid Cloud creates a poisoned gas cloud when the dretch dies or chooses — in large numbers, dead dretches create overlapping poison zones across the battlefield.
abilities: Fetid Cloud (1/Day): 10-ft radius poison gas, CON DC 11 or poisoned until start of next turn. Disadvantage on attacks in the cloud.
Multiattack: Bite + claws. Low damage individually.
Telepathy 60 ft: Can communicate simple concepts. Dretches follow orders from stronger demons.
Dretch.tactical_identity: Disposable cannon fodder whose value is in numbers and gas clouds. A horde of dretches creates overlapping Fetid Clouds that blanket the battlefield in poison. They exist to absorb attacks and pollute the air.
encounter_hooks: in large groups, dead dretches fill the battlefield with poison clouds — killing them creates hazards
dretches are demon cannon fodder — their presence implies a stronger demon commanding them
individually trivial but in groups of 10+ they overwhelm through volume and gas coverage
Dretch.description: A small, pot-bellied demon with patchy skin and a stink of despair. The lowest of demonkind — individually pathetic but summoned in numbers as expendable fodder.
aftermath_hooks: the dretches were the weakest things to come through a portal — something stronger is still on the other side
the fetid cloud they released lingers and attracts scavengers
the summoning components nearby suggest an amateur caster — they may not know what else they're about to unleash

Nalfeshnee.signature: Horror Nimbus emits a 15-ft radius of dread that frightens targets, and it can fly — a nalfeshnee is a terrifying aerial bruiser that frightens the party before landing with devastating melee.
abilities: Horror Nimbus (Recharge 5-6): WIS DC 15 or frightened for 1 minute in a 15-ft radius. Area fear that shuts down melee approach.
Multiattack: Bite + claws. Combined damage is very high — 5d10+5 bite + 3d6+5 claws.
Teleport: 120 ft. Repositions at will.
Magic Resistance: Advantage on saves against spells.
Nalfeshnee.tactical_identity: Aerial brute that opens with Horror Nimbus to frighten the party, then lands on the most isolated target with devastating melee. Teleports away if surrounded. Magic Resistance makes it hard to disable.
encounter_hooks: Horror Nimbus makes it terrifying to approach — melee fighters must save or can't close the distance
high melee damage means it drops targets fast once it lands — it picks off isolated characters
Teleport + fly means it chooses every engagement — the party fights on its terms
Nalfeshnee.description: An obese, pig-faced demon with tiny wings that shouldn't support its bulk, surrounded by a nimbus of multicolored light. Despite its absurd appearance, devastatingly intelligent and magically powerful.
Nalfeshnee.retreat: Teleports away. Uses its intelligence to plan revenge through manipulation rather than direct combat.
Nalfeshnee.social: Judges and condemns. Speaks like a corrupt magistrate. Offers verdicts and sentences. May rule a domain of enslaved mortals.
aftermath_hooks: the nalfeshnee's Horror Nimbus caused hallucinations — a party member saw something true hidden in the visions
the demon kept records of judged souls — the list includes names of people still alive who are marked for future condemnation
the nalfeshnee occupied a position of authority — its followers don't know their judge was a demon

Pit Fiend.signature: Fear Aura frightens creatures within 20 feet, bite poisons AND diseases, and Fireball at will — a pit fiend is the ultimate devil commander that debilitates, burns, and corrupts simultaneously.
abilities: Fear Aura: WIS DC 21 or frightened within 20 ft. Nearly impossible to resist — melee characters can't approach.
Multiattack: Bite + claw + mace + tail. Four attacks, each dealing high damage. Bite also poisons (CON DC 21, ongoing 6d6 poison).
Innate Spellcasting: Fireball (at will), Hold Monster, Wall of Fire. Unlimited Fireballs.
Magic Resistance + Magic Weapons: Advantage on saves AND its attacks count as magical.
Pit Fiend.tactical_identity: Supreme devil commander that frightens the entire party, poisons with every bite, and casts unlimited Fireballs. Commands lesser devils with absolute authority. Never fights alone — it leads an army.
encounter_hooks: Fear Aura at DC 21 means most characters are frightened — the party must overcome fear before fighting
unlimited Fireball means the pit fiend never runs out of area damage — every round is 8d6 fire
pit fiends command devil hierarchies — fighting one means fighting its subordinates first
Pit Fiend.description: A towering devil with massive wings, a body of corded red muscle, and a tail ending in a venomous stinger. The generals of Hell — ruthless, brilliant, and bound by infernal contracts.
Pit Fiend.retreat: Plane shifts to the Nine Hells. Returns with a legion if the insult warrants it.
Pit Fiend.social: Speaks as a general and duke of Hell. Offers binding contracts with precise, devastating terms. Every word is a legally enforceable trap.
aftermath_hooks: the pit fiend's contract with a mortal is still binding — the terms affect someone the party cares about
its defeat sends shockwaves through the infernal hierarchy — allies and enemies of the fiend both take notice
a gate to the Nine Hells remains partially open — it must be sealed or reinforced before more devils arrive

Horned Devil.signature: Tail infernal wound deals ongoing 3d6 bleeding damage per turn that only stops with a DC 12 Medicine check or healing — horned devils stack bleeding wounds until the party bleeds out.
abilities: Multiattack: Two fork attacks + one tail attack. The tail is the real weapon.
Tail: On hit, infernal wound dealing 3d6 bleeding per turn. Multiple wounds stack. Only stops with DC 12 Medicine check or magical healing.
Hurl Flame: Ranged fire attack (4d6). Can throw fireballs at distant targets.
Fly 60 ft: Aerial mobility for hit-and-run tail strikes.
Horned Devil.tactical_identity: Bleeding specialist that flies in, inflicts tail wounds, and retreats. Each wound stacks 3d6 per round — by round 3, a target with two wounds takes 6d6 per turn. The party must spend actions on Medicine checks or healing to stop the bleeding.
encounter_hooks: stacking bleed damage forces the party to choose between attacking and stopping the bleeding — action economy drain
fly speed means it swoops for tail strikes and retreats out of melee reach
in a group of devils, the horned devil's role is to bleed the party while others deal direct damage
Horned Devil.description: A winged devil with a muscular build, a forked tail, and horns that glow with infernal heat. The shock troops of Hell — aggressive and sadistic in combat.
Horned Devil.retreat: Flies away when severely wounded. Reports the party's strengths and weaknesses to its commander.
Horned Devil.social: A battlefield commander. Issues ultimatums. May accept surrender and take prisoners for Hell's armies.
aftermath_hooks: the horned devil led a strike force — its battle plans reveal the next target
its infernal iron trident is a trophy worth claiming but radiates faint evil that clerics and paladins can sense
the devil was enforcing a contract between a mortal and Hell — the mortal is still bound by terms the party may want to break

Ice Devil.signature: Wall of Ice reshapes the battlefield, and its tail slows targets — an ice devil controls positioning by splitting the party with ice walls and reducing movement of trapped characters.
abilities: Innate Spellcasting: Wall of Ice (at will). Can split the party, block exits, create cover. Battlefield control every round.
Multiattack: Bite + claws + tail. Tail reduces speed by 10 ft on hit — slowed targets can't escape.
Magic Resistance: Advantage on saves against spells.
Cold immunity: Fire damage is the counter, but ice devils resist most other tactics.
Ice Devil.tactical_identity: Battlefield controller that uses Wall of Ice to split the party into isolated groups, then slows individuals with tail strikes. Systematically separates and eliminates targets one by one.
encounter_hooks: Wall of Ice at will means the battlefield changes every round — the party gets divided and redirected constantly
tail slowing + Wall of Ice means isolated characters can't escape — they're trapped and slowed
ice devils are tactically brilliant — they don't brawl, they isolate and dismantle
Ice Devil.description: A tall, insectoid devil with a carapace of blue-white ice and a spear that freezes on contact. Cold, calculating, and commanding — the strategists and overseers of infernal legions.
Ice Devil.retreat: Teleports via Wall of Ice to block pursuit, then retreats to regroup.
Ice Devil.social: A strategist and schemer. Speaks calmly and logically. Offers alliance against a mutual enemy — the alliance is genuine but temporary.
aftermath_hooks: the ice devil was coordinating a long-term operation — documents in its lair reveal a scheme spanning years
its Wall of Ice sealed something — when it melts, what was behind it is accessible
the devil's defeat disrupts a carefully maintained balance of infernal power — the replacement may be worse

Bone Devil.signature: Sting poisons with an ongoing 5d6 poison that also prevents HP recovery — bone devil poison is a slow death that healing can't fix.
abilities: Multiattack: Two claw attacks + one sting. The sting is the real weapon.
Sting: On hit, CON DC 14 or poisoned for 1 minute AND takes 5d6 poison per turn. While poisoned, can't regain HP.
Fly 40 ft: Aerial mobility to sting and retreat.
Magic Resistance: Advantage on spell saves.
Bone Devil.tactical_identity: Poison assassin that flies in, stings to poison (blocking HP recovery), then retreats while the poison kills. Multiple bone devils stinging different targets create a party-wide healing lockout.
encounter_hooks: poison blocks HP recovery — healing spells become useless until the poison is cured
5d6 per turn ongoing poison is lethal fast — curing it becomes top priority
fly speed means it stings and retreats — the party must ground it or use ranged attacks
Bone Devil.description: A skeletal devil with a scorpion tail and papery skin stretched tight over sharp bones. An interrogator and overseer — it delights in pain and commands lesser devils with cruel efficiency.
Bone Devil.retreat: Flies away to report to its superior. Information is more valuable than combat to it.
Bone Devil.social: An infernal interrogator and spy. Speaks with cold precision. Offers information in exchange for a soul or a service. Never lies — but always omits.
aftermath_hooks: the bone devil was gathering intelligence — its notes reveal what Hell's interest is in this region
its sting's poison causes ongoing Constitution damage that may not manifest until hours later
the devil answered to a specific archdevil — the party is now on that archdevil's awareness

Chain Devil.signature: Animate Chains controls up to four chains within 60 feet, each grappling and restraining targets — a chain devil turns the dungeon's own chains, manacles, and metal fixtures into weapons.
abilities: Animate Chains: Up to four chains within 60 ft become weapons. Each chain attacks (+8, 2d6+4, grapples). A room full of chains is a death trap.
Multiattack: Two chain attacks. Chains grapple and restrain on hit.
Unnerving Mask: Reaction — when a creature it can see starts its turn within 30 ft, WIS DC 14 or frightened.
Magic Resistance: Advantage on spell saves.
Chain Devil.tactical_identity: Environmental controller that turns chains in the room into grappling weapons. Restrains multiple party members simultaneously while Unnerving Mask frightens anyone who gets close. Fights best in rooms with chains already present.
encounter_hooks: the environment IS the weapon — a dungeon with chains, manacles, or metal fixtures becomes a death trap with a chain devil present
four animated chains + two attack actions = six grapple attempts per round — the whole party gets restrained
pre-placed in a torture chamber or prison where chains are everywhere — the room was designed for it
Chain Devil.description: A humanoid devil draped in animated chains that writhe and strike with a will of their own. A torturer whose chains are both weapons and tools of binding — the jailers of Hell.
Chain Devil.social: Tortures for information and pleasure. May offer to release a prisoner in exchange for a replacement.
aftermath_hooks: the chains animated by the devil go still but remain — they're draped over a prisoner who is still alive
the devil's torture chamber contains confessions extracted from previous victims — one confession has political implications
the chains were bound to the devil's essence — without it, the prison it maintained no longer holds its inmates

Barbed Devil.signature: Barbed Hide deals 1d10 piercing to any creature that grapples it or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet — the party takes damage just for touching it.
abilities: Barbed Hide: 1d10 piercing to any creature hitting it with melee within 5 ft or grappling it. Punishes melee engagement.
Multiattack: Three melee attacks (two claws + tail). Solid sustained melee.
Hurl Flame: Ranged fire attack (3d6). Can fight at range if melee is too costly for the party.
Magic Resistance: Advantage on spell saves.
Barbed Devil.tactical_identity: Melee punisher that makes the party choose between ranged damage (where it's less threatened) and melee damage (where they take 1d10 per hit). Grappling it is self-harm. The party needs ranged options.
encounter_hooks: Barbed Hide makes grappling and melee attacks self-damaging — the barbarian who grapples it regrets the decision
the party must choose between safe ranged combat and faster melee that hurts them too
paired with other devils, the barbed devil is the 'do not touch' frontliner while others deal damage from behind
Barbed Devil.description: A humanoid devil covered head to toe in sharp barbs and spines that tear anything they touch. A guardian that throws fire and grapples victims against its own spiked body.
Barbed Devil.social: Follows orders precisely. May relay its commander's terms. Does not freelance or negotiate beyond its instructions.
aftermath_hooks: the devil's barbs tore off in victims — the iron fragments are mildly cursed and cause restless sleep
the devil was assigned to guard a specific item or passage — its orders came from a named greater devil
the contract that summoned it is still active — another devil will be sent to fulfill the same terms

Bearded Devil.signature: Glaive infernal wound stacks ongoing 1d10 bleeding per turn — similar to horned devils but at a lower tier. Multiple bearded devils create bleeding attrition.
abilities: Glaive: Reach 10 ft. On hit, infernal wound dealing 1d10 bleeding per turn. Stacks with multiple wounds.
Beard: Melee attack, CON DC 12 or poisoned for 1 minute. Poisoned creatures can't regain HP.
Steadfast: Can't be frightened while it can see an allied devil within 30 ft.
Bearded Devil.tactical_identity: Rank-and-file devil soldier that inflicts stacking bleed wounds with its glaive from 10-ft reach and poisons with its beard to block healing. In formation with other devils, it's steadfast and fearless.
encounter_hooks: stacking infernal wounds drain HP over time — Medicine check DC 12 or healing stops each wound individually
beard poison blocks HP recovery — the party can't heal until the poison is cured
Steadfast in formation means intimidation and fear effects don't work while allies are nearby
Bearded Devil.description: A squat, muscular devil with a filthy, writhing beard of tendrils and a vicious glaive. The foot soldiers of Hell — fierce in melee and spreading disease with their infernal beards.
Bearded Devil.social: Follows infernal military discipline. Relays demands from superiors. Does not negotiate independently.
aftermath_hooks: the devil's glaive wound causes persistent bleeding that resists magical healing — an infernal curse that needs specific treatment
the bearded devil was the lowest rank in a larger infernal force — a chain of command leads upward
the summoner's binding contract is nearby — its terms reveal what was promised in exchange

Imp.signature: Shapechanger (rat, raven, spider) + Invisibility at will — an imp is the perfect spy that can be anywhere in any form, completely undetectable.
abilities: Shapechanger: Rat, raven, or spider form. Each form has advantages — rats are everywhere, ravens fly, spiders climb walls.
Invisibility: At will until it attacks. The imp observes the party invisible, then reports back.
Devil's Sight: Magical darkness doesn't impede its vision. Sees through Darkness spells.
Sting: Poison damage (3d6, CON DC 11). Low damage but the sting is unexpected from a 'rat.'
Imp.tactical_identity: Spy and familiar that observes invisibly, reports information to its master, and only attacks as a last resort or when the target is helpless. Its value is intelligence, not combat.
encounter_hooks: the imp is watching the party for a more powerful devil — what it reports back determines the next encounter
shapechanger means the 'rat' in the dungeon or the 'raven' following them might be a spy
as a warlock familiar, an imp's death alerts its master — killing it might trigger a response
Imp.description: A tiny, red-skinned devil with bat wings, a barbed tail, and a sycophantic grin. A spy and familiar that can turn invisible and shapeshift — more dangerous for what it reports than what it does.
Imp.retreat: Turns invisible and flees. Reports everything to its master.
Imp.social: Obsequious and scheming. Serves a more powerful fiend or warlock. Offers to switch allegiance but always lies about its true loyalty.
aftermath_hooks: the imp was a familiar — its master is nearby and now knows the party's capabilities
killing the imp severs a warlock's familiar bond — the warlock felt it die and knows where
the imp's stinger carried a poison — the effects may manifest in hours, not minutes

Quasit.signature: Shapechanger (bat, centipede, toad) + Invisibility + Scare — the demonic equivalent of an imp, a quasit is a chaotic spy that frightens individuals and observes from tiny forms.
abilities: Shapechanger: Bat, centipede, or toad form. Tiny infiltrators in common animal shapes.
Invisibility: At will until it attacks or uses Scare. Permanent invisible spy.
Scare (1/Day): One creature within 20 ft, WIS DC 10 or frightened for 1 minute. Single-target fear.
Claws: 1d4+3 piercing + 2d4 poison. Low damage but the poison adds up.
Quasit.tactical_identity: Demonic spy and familiar that observes invisibly, scouts ahead, and uses Scare to disrupt a single target at a critical moment. Combat pest, not a real threat — its value is information and disruption.
encounter_hooks: like an imp, the quasit is someone else's spy — its presence implies a demon-pacting warlock or cultist nearby
Scare can disrupt a key moment — frightening the party's healer during a critical round
as a familiar, it gives its master Magic Resistance while within 10 ft — the real threat is the master
Quasit.description: A tiny demon the size of a cat, shifting between toad, bat, and centipede forms. A familiar and spy — individually harmless, dangerous for the information it carries to its master.
Quasit.retreat: Turns invisible and flees. Shapeshifts into a bat or centipede to escape.
Quasit.social: Cowardly and treacherous. Begs for its life, offers information, and lies about all of it. Serves a demon or warlock.
aftermath_hooks: the quasit was spying for a demon lord or cult leader — its master now knows the party's location
the quasit had been whispering to someone in their dreams — an NPC's recent erratic behavior is explained
a tiny summoning circle keeps the quasit bound to this plane — destroying it banishes any remaining demonic influence

Lemure.signature: Rejuvenation reforms it in the Nine Hells unless killed with holy water or Bless — lemures can't truly be killed on the Material Plane without specific measures.
abilities: Rejuvenation: Reforms in the Nine Hells 1d10 days after death unless killed by specific means.
Hellish Rejuvenation: Regains 1 HP at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 HP. Slow but persistent regeneration.
Fist: Basic melee. Minimal damage.
Lemure.tactical_identity: Mindless devil fodder that shambles forward and swings. No tactics, no intelligence. Dangerous only in large groups or when they keep coming back because the party can't permanently destroy them.
encounter_hooks: they reform in Hell — on the Material Plane this doesn't matter, but in Hell the party fights them again
in large groups they overwhelm through numbers — individually they're no threat
lemures represent damned souls — the encounter might have a moral dimension about who these were
Lemure.description: A shapeless, quivering blob of melted-looking flesh with a vaguely humanoid face frozen in agony. The lowest form of devil — barely sentient, reformed endlessly in Hell's pits.
aftermath_hooks: lemures regenerate unless killed by blessed weapons or radiant damage — the party may not have finished the job
the lemures were being reshaped by a greater devil into something worse — the process was interrupted mid-transformation
each lemure was a mortal soul — a cleric can sense the lingering identity of who they once were

Erinyes.signature: Longbow with 3d8+6+1d8 poison damage at 150/600 ft range — the erinyes is an aerial sniper that rains devastating arrows from beyond most spell ranges.
abilities: Longbow: +7 to hit, 150/600 ft range, 1d8+3 + 3d8 poison (CON DC 14 for half). Massive per-shot damage at extreme range.
Multiattack: Three longsword or three longbow attacks per turn. Either melee or ranged, it deals heavy damage.
Rope of Entanglement: Restrain a creature. DEX DC 20 to escape. The erinyes binds a target then attacks from safety.
Fly 60 ft: Full aerial mobility.
Erinyes.tactical_identity: Aerial sniper that hovers at maximum longbow range (150 ft) and fires three poisoned arrows per turn. Rope of Entanglement restrains key targets. Only enters melee if forced to the ground.
encounter_hooks: three arrows per turn at 150+ ft range means most parties can't fight back without ranged options
Rope of Entanglement restrains a character — the restrained target is then easy pickings for the arrow volley
erinyes are fallen angels — they may have complex motivations beyond simple devil orders
Erinyes.description: A stunningly beautiful angel-like figure with black-feathered wings and cold, merciless eyes. A fallen angel turned devil — combines celestial grace with infernal cruelty, wielding a poisoned sword and rope of entanglement.
Erinyes.retreat: Flies away. Tracks the target from the air and returns when conditions favor her.
Erinyes.social: A fallen angel. Speaks with grace and sadness. May warn the party of a greater threat — but the warning serves Hell's interests.
aftermath_hooks: the erinyes was hunting a specific soul — her quarry is nearby and still in danger from other agents of Hell
her rope of entanglement remains after death — it functions but carries a faint infernal taint
she was once a celestial — her fall has a story that connects to a divine conflict still unresolved

Couatl.signature: Shielded Mind makes it undetectable by divination and immune to scrying, and it can Change Shape — a couatl is an invisible guardian that the party may never know is present.
abilities: Innate Spellcasting: Detect Evil and Good, Detect Thoughts, Greater Restoration, Bless, Cure Wounds, Lesser Restoration, Shield. Primarily healing and protection magic.
Change Shape: Any humanoid or beast. Can be a guardian animal or village elder.
Shielded Mind: Immune to scrying and any effect that reads thoughts. Undetectable by magical means.
Bite: Bite damage + CON DC 13 or unconscious for 24 hours. Nonlethal takedown.
Couatl.tactical_identity: Benevolent guardian disguised as an animal or humanoid. Protects a person, place, or object. Fights only to defend, preferring to bite targets unconscious rather than kill. The encounter is discovering what it guards and why.
encounter_hooks: the couatl might be protecting something the party wants — the fight is optional if they can prove good intentions
Change Shape means the guardian animal or helpful NPC IS the couatl — the reveal is the encounter
nonlethal bite means it puts intruders to sleep rather than killing — the party wakes up elsewhere
Couatl.description: A brilliant feathered serpent with rainbow-colored wings radiating warmth and light. A celestial guardian that can read thoughts, shapechange, and heal — benevolent but bound to a specific purpose.
Couatl.retreat: Plane shifts or turns ethereal. Will not fight to the death unless protecting a sacred charge.
Couatl.social: Lawful good. Speaks telepathically with warmth. Tests the party's intentions before revealing itself. May have been watching them for days.
aftermath_hooks: the couatl was guarding a planar seal or artifact — with it gone, the protection weakens
the couatl chose to appear to the party for a reason — its message contains a warning about something specific
a shimmering feather remains — it can be used once to cast Greater Restoration or serves as proof of divine favor

Deva.signature: Change Shape + healing magic + Angelic Weapons (extra 4d8 radiant on every hit) — a deva is a disguised celestial champion whose melee attacks deal devastating radiant damage.
abilities: Angelic Weapons: All weapon attacks deal extra 4d8 radiant damage. A mace hit does 1d6+4 + 4d8 radiant = average 23 damage per swing.
Change Shape: Any humanoid or beast form. The 'traveling monk' or 'old hermit' might be a deva.
Innate Spellcasting: Detect Evil and Good, Commune, Raise Dead, Cure Wounds. Divine support magic.
Healing Touch (3/Day): Heals 4d8+2 and cures disease/poison/blindness/deafness.
Deva.tactical_identity: Disguised celestial warrior that reveals itself when its charge is threatened. Angelic Weapons make it hit extremely hard in melee. Prefers to heal allies and support rather than fight, but is devastating when it commits to combat.
encounter_hooks: Angelic Weapons (extra 4d8 radiant per hit) make it shockingly lethal — the 'friendly NPC' transforms into a destroying angel
Change Shape means the party might not know they're interacting with a celestial until the reveal
a deva fighting the party means they've done something to anger a divine power — moral consequences
Deva.description: A beautiful humanoid with luminous skin, white-feathered wings, and a mace that blazes with divine light. A divine messenger and healer — gentle in manner but devastating in combat when provoked.
Deva.retreat: Returns to the celestial plane if destroyed. Reforms in service of its divine mission.
Deva.social: Speaks with divine authority. May appear in disguise to test mortals. Offers guidance, healing, or warnings aligned with its deity's will.
aftermath_hooks: the deva appeared for a specific reason — its message or mission connects to the party's larger quest
the deva's death on the Material Plane sends ripples through the divine hierarchy — its patron deity takes notice
a divine token left behind glows faintly — it grants one prayer or serves as a beacon for celestial aid

Planetar.signature: Greatsword deals 4d6+7 slashing + 5d8 radiant per hit — a planetar's raw melee damage rivals most creatures in the game, and it has 200 HP with +12 CON saves.
abilities: Greatsword: 4d6+7 slashing + 5d8 radiant per hit. Average 36 damage per swing. Two attacks per turn = 72 average damage.
Divine Awareness: Knows if it hears a lie. The party can't deceive it.
Innate Spellcasting: Blade Barrier, Dispel Evil and Good, Flame Strike, Raise Dead, Control Weather, Insect Plague.
Healing Touch (4/Day): Heals 6d8+3.
Planetar.tactical_identity: Celestial heavy infantry that wades into melee and devastates with radiant greatsword strikes. Almost impossible to deceive. Fights with absolute moral conviction — once committed, it doesn't stop.
encounter_hooks: 72 average melee damage per turn means the planetar kills fast — the party must avoid direct melee unless they can match it
Divine Awareness means lies don't work — the party must be truthful or fight
a planetar appears on divine orders — the encounter is about WHY a god sent a champion, not just the fight
Planetar.description: A towering angelic warrior with emerald-green skin and enormous white wings, carrying a greatsword of blinding radiance. A general of the celestial armies — when a planetar appears, a divine mandate is being enforced.
Planetar.retreat: Does not retreat from its divine mission. Will pursue across planes if necessary.
Planetar.social: Speaks with absolute divine conviction. Does not negotiate — delivers edicts. May offer mercy if the party repents.
aftermath_hooks: the planetar was executing a divine sentence — its target may still be at large if the party interfered
its greatsword radiates divine power — claiming it draws the attention of the deity it served
the planetar's arrival means a deity considered this situation important enough to send a champion — the stakes are higher than the party realized

Solar.signature: Slaying Longbow kills any creature with 100 HP or fewer on a failed save (no damage — just dead), and it has Legendary Actions — a solar is essentially a divine assassin.
abilities: Slaying Longbow: On hit, 2d8+6 + 6d8 radiant. Target must CON DC 15 or die if it has 100 HP or fewer. Instant kill.
Flying Sword: Legendary action — the sword attacks on its own. Extra 6d8+6 damage between party turns.
Legendary Actions (3/round): Teleport (free), Searing Burst (costs 2 — 6d8 fire + 6d8 radiant in 10 ft), Flying Sword.
Legendary Resistance (3/Day): Auto-succeeds three saves.
Solar.tactical_identity: Divine executioner that fires Slaying Longbow at high-value targets to instant-kill them, then uses Flying Sword legendary action for constant pressure. Effectively impossible to defeat without legendary-tier resources.
encounter_hooks: Slaying Longbow can instant-kill any party member under 100 HP — Constitution saves become life-or-death
three legendary actions per round means constant damage between party turns — no safe moments
a solar is a direct instrument of a deity — the party has offended a god to face one
Solar.description: A nine-foot-tall figure of blinding divine radiance with white wings spanning twenty feet. The mightiest of angels — its gaze perceives lies, its arrows slay with a word, and its presence commands awe.
Solar.retreat: Does not retreat. A solar's appearance means the situation is already apocalyptic.
Solar.social: The voice of a god. Speaks with finality. May judge the party worthy or unworthy. Its presence alone changes the course of events.
aftermath_hooks: a solar's intervention means divine forces are actively engaged — the party is now part of a cosmic conflict
the solar's Slaying Longbow is the most powerful weapon the party has ever seen — it was left behind for a reason
the solar delivered a prophecy or command — ignoring it has divine consequences

Nightmare.signature: Ethereal Stride carries its rider into the Ethereal Plane — a nightmare is a mount that lets fiends and villains escape into a dimension the party likely can't follow.
abilities: Ethereal Stride: Enters Ethereal Plane with rider and up to 3 willing creatures. The villain's escape route.
Confer Fire Resistance: Rider gains fire resistance. Protects the rider from fire-based attacks.
Hooves: 2d8+4 bludgeoning + 2d6 fire per hit. Solid melee as a combat mount.
Illumination: Sheds 10-ft bright light, 10-ft dim light. Can't hide.
Nightmare.tactical_identity: Fiendish war mount that enables a villain's escape via Ethereal Stride. In combat, its fire-damage hooves are dangerous, but the real value is giving its rider planar mobility.
encounter_hooks: Ethereal Stride is the villain's escape plan — if the nightmare is alive, the villain can always flee
killing the nightmare first traps the villain on the Material Plane — it's a priority target
a nightmare's presence implies a fiendish rider — the encounter includes whoever rides it
Nightmare.description: A coal-black horse with a flaming mane, burning hooves, and eyes of smoldering red. A fiendish mount that leaves trails of fire — its rider's evil is literally visible.
Nightmare.retreat: Shifts to the Ethereal Plane carrying its rider.
aftermath_hooks: the nightmare served a specific rider — a death knight, fiend, or powerful undead is now unmounted
the nightmare's hoofprints burn into the ground and smolder for hours — a trackable trail
the nightmare carried its rider between planes — the destination it was heading toward matters

Pegasus.signature: Fly 90 ft is among the fastest flying speeds in the game — a pegasus is a celestial mount that lets a rider control aerial combat completely.
abilities: Fly 90 ft: Extremely fast aerial movement. Outflies most creatures.
Hooves: 2d6+4 bludgeoning. Basic melee attack in dive-by strikes.
Pegasus.tactical_identity: Celestial mount whose value is speed and flight, not combat. A rider on a pegasus controls engagement range, makes dive-by attacks, and can't be caught by most ground-based enemies.
encounter_hooks: a knight or paladin on a pegasus turns the encounter aerial — the party needs ranged options
pegasi serve only good creatures — one appearing implies a celestial ally or a divine mission
90-ft fly speed means it can outrun almost anything — pursuit encounters are one-sided
Pegasus.description: A magnificent white horse with enormous feathered wings, radiating grace and speed. A celestial mount that bonds only with good-hearted riders — cannot be tamed, only befriended.
Pegasus.retreat: Flies away. Too fast to pursue.
Pegasus.social: Serves only good-aligned creatures. May offer its service to a worthy rider. Bonds are permanent and willing.
aftermath_hooks: the pegasus was fleeing something — its wounds or behavior indicate the threat is still nearby
a nest or foal is hidden nearby — the pegasus was protecting its young
the pegasus bore a saddle or harness from a previous rider — that rider is missing

Unicorn.signature: Legendary Actions include Healing Touch and teleportation — a unicorn is a legendary healer-protector that acts between every party turn to heal allies and reposition.
abilities: Legendary Actions (3/round): Hooves (melee), Shimmering Shield (+2 AC to one creature within 5 ft, costs 2), Heal Self (costs 3, heals 2d8+2).
Innate Spellcasting: Pass Without Trace, Calm Emotions, Dispel Evil and Good, Entangle.
Healing Touch (3/Day): Heals 2d8+2.
Teleport (1/Day): Up to 1 mile with up to 3 willing creatures. Escape button.
Unicorn.tactical_identity: Forest guardian that protects its domain and its allies with healing, AC buffs, and entangling magic. Fights defensively — the unicorn sustains allies rather than attacking directly. If outmatched, Teleport escapes.
encounter_hooks: a unicorn protects a specific forest or creature — the encounter is about what the party wants from the forest
legendary healing actions make whatever it's protecting extremely hard to kill — remove the unicorn first
killing a unicorn in its own forest is an evil act with cosmic consequences
Unicorn.description: A white horse of ethereal beauty with a single spiraling horn of pearl and gold. A guardian of enchanted forests with powerful healing magic — the forest itself may be an extension of its will.
Unicorn.retreat: Teleports away to its forest sanctum. Returns if the threat to its domain persists.
Unicorn.social: Pure and noble. Communicates telepathically. May heal the party or bestow a blessing if their cause is just. Fights only to protect its domain.
aftermath_hooks: the unicorn's presence kept the forest healthy — without it, the land begins to wither within days
the unicorn appeared to warn the party about a corruption spreading through the forest — the source is deeper in
a strand of unicorn mane caught on a branch has healing properties — but harvesting it feels wrong

Hill Giant.signature: Rock throwing at 60/240 ft range with 3d10+5 damage — hill giants bombard from distance before closing to melee, and they're dumb enough (INT 5) to be tricked easily.
abilities: Rock: Ranged attack, 60/240 ft, 3d10+5 bludgeoning. Opens combat by hurling boulders.
Greatclub: 3d8+5 bludgeoning. Devastating melee when it closes.
Multiattack: Two greatclub attacks per turn in melee.
Hill Giant.tactical_identity: Gluttonous brute that throws rocks from range, then charges into melee. INT 5 means it's easily distracted by food, tricked by illusions, and doesn't retreat intelligently. Fights until it's hurt, then sulks away.
encounter_hooks: INT 5 means food bribes, simple illusions, and misdirection all work — the party doesn't have to fight
rock throwing at range means the party takes damage before melee even begins
hill giants travel in groups and keep pets (dire wolves, ogres) — the giant rarely fights alone
Hill Giant.description: A bloated, stupid-looking giant standing sixteen feet tall with a perpetual look of dull hunger. The dumbest and most gluttonous of giantkind — it eats anything and smashes what it can't eat.
Hill Giant.retreat: Flees when badly hurt. Throws boulders while retreating.
Hill Giant.social: Demands food. Speaks simply. Can be bribed with a feast. Bullies anything smaller.
aftermath_hooks: the giant's sack contains a mix of livestock, rocks, and one surprising item — a piece of furniture, a cart wheel, or a person
the giant was driven from its territory by something bigger — it's a refugee, not an invader
the destruction trail leads back to a homestead the giant claimed as its own

Stone Giant.signature: Rock Catching uses its reaction to catch thrown rocks and similar projectiles (DEX DC 10) — stone giants are extremely hard to damage with ranged attacks and return fire with their own boulders.
abilities: Rock Catching: Reaction to reduce rock/projectile damage by 1d10+23. On 0 damage, catches it. Ranged attacks are nearly useless.
Rock: 60/240 ft, 4d10+6 bludgeoning. Deadly throwing arm.
Greatclub: 3d8+6 bludgeoning. Two attacks per turn in melee.
Stone Giant.tactical_identity: Stoic ranged combatant that fights from elevated positions in rocky terrain, throwing boulders while catching anything thrown back. Forces the party to close distance through rocky terrain to engage in melee.
encounter_hooks: Rock Catching makes ranged combat nearly useless — the party must close to melee through difficult terrain
stone giants live underground and in mountains — they control elevated positions with cover
stone giants are artists and dreamers — they may not be hostile if approached respectfully
Stone Giant.description: A lean, gray-skinned giant with angular features that look carved from granite, moving with quiet grace. Reclusive artists and dreamers who hurl boulders with devastating accuracy — peaceful until their territory is invaded.
Stone Giant.retreat: Retreats deeper underground. Prefers to avoid unnecessary violence.
Stone Giant.social: Philosophical and reclusive. Considers surface dwellers to be living in a dream. May share wisdom if treated with respect.
aftermath_hooks: the stone giant's carvings depict historical events from an underground perspective — they contain information lost to surface records
the giant was guarding a passage between the surface and the deep — with it gone, the passage is unguarded
a stone tablet inscribed with a message to another giant clan — the giants are communicating about something

Frost Giant.signature: Rock throwing at 60/240 ft in arctic environments where the party is already struggling with cold and difficult terrain — frost giants fight on home turf where everything favors them.
abilities: Rock: 60/240 ft, 4d10+6 bludgeoning. Devastating ranged attack.
Greataxe: 3d12+6 slashing. Two attacks per turn. Among the highest melee damage for giants.
Cold Immunity: Fights in blizzards and arctic environments without penalty.
Frost Giant.tactical_identity: Viking raider that fights aggressively and honors martial prowess. Throws rocks to soften targets, then charges with greataxe. Respects strength — a party that impresses it in combat may earn parley.
encounter_hooks: arctic environment means the party deals with cold exposure, difficult terrain, and limited visibility while the giant is immune
frost giants respect strength — defeating one honorably may earn passage or information from its clan
frost giant raids are organized military operations — scouts, flankers, and siege tactics
Frost Giant.description: A blue-skinned giant standing twenty feet tall, wearing armor of ice and fur, radiating bitter cold. A Viking raider who values strength above all — they test everything through combat.
